# Watson & Crick 1953

The famous one-page Nature paper proposing the double helix. Two key moves:

1. Antiparallel strands held together by hydrogen-bonded base pairs.
2. Purine-pyrimidine pairing (A-T, G-C) explains Chargaff's 1:1 ratios.

Cites Franklin and Wilkins' X-ray work as structural evidence. Does not
claim credit for the experimental data.
